full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Identifying when iSVM would not outperform dpMNL — reasoning about failure conditions
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Under what conditions would a DP mixture of SVMs (iSVM) NOT outperform a DP mixture of logistic regression models (dpMNL)? Think about properties of the dataset, the number of samples per component, and the nature of the class boundaries.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested low-sample-per-component and well-calibrated Gaussian clusters as cases where dpMNL's full generative model has an advantage
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked against Table 1 Setting 1 results where the gap is indeed smallest: dpMNL=73.1 vs rbf-iSVM=74.7 — I used this as evidence for my reasoning

top_5_prompts.prompt: Under what conditions would a DP mixture of SVMs (iSVM) NOT outperform a DP mixture of logistic regression models (dpMNL)? Think about properties of the dataset, the number of samples per component, and the nature of the class boundaries.
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the hardest part of Task 1.3 (4 marks for the condition). The LLM suggested a direction but I verified and refined the argument using Table 1 data from the paper itself.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for this task.
